title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 15, 2026
headline: Anthropic's flagship safety pledge reversal becomes the first canonical 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' event; ten new methodology categories documented; Russia's death toll in a single overnight strike revised 1 to 24; India's naval Rohingya deportation triggers a second consecutive non-accountability finding.
summary: May 15 was the methodology's most consequential single-night hold-release cycle: three major entities (Anthropic, Microsoft, Google/Alphabet) came off hold simultaneously, generating five score changes, ten new conduct categories, and four boundary-watch flags. The defining event is Anthropic's RSP v3 pre-deployment safety pledge drop (February 26) — the first cleanly documented 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' in the AI labs cluster, producing a boundary-direction downgrade from 60.0 to 58.1. Simultaneously, xAI's Pentagon classified access-without-restrictions vs. Anthropic's exclusion for maintaining limits establishes a 'perverse-procurement-incentive' sector signal that now informs SYS/INT scoring across all labs competing in military contracting. India documents its second consecutive non-accountability for a naval Rohingya deportation (May 8, 2026: 38–40 UNHCR-registered refugees forced into the Andaman Sea), generating the 'maritime-deportation-without-due-process' conduct category and a further -2.4 downgrade to 28.1. Meta AI's EU DSA preliminary breach finding on under-13 access, combined with four concurrent US–EU accountability mechanisms, produces a -3.1 downgrade to 26.3 and confirms a sector-wide AI-chatbot-child-safety-failure pattern alongside xAI. Russia's May 14–15 mass strike death toll was revised from 1 to 24 civilians killed; five floor entities hold at 0.0 with expanded conduct documentation. Google/Alphabet sits at the exact Developing/Functional band boundary (40.0). Two active holds remain (OpenAI through May 21, Open Bionics math-hygiene 13 cycles), and the Musk v. Altman jury begins deliberations Monday May 18.

pipeline.cycleType: HOLD-RELEASE TRIPLE + FLOOR CONDUCT DOCS + ROTATION

topSignals.title: Anthropic Boundary-Down: Safety Pledge Reversal Under Government Pressure — First Canonical Instance
topSignals.description: RSP v3 (February 26) dropped Anthropic's pre-deployment safety guarantee — the most-cited Anthropic governance differentiator — under documented Pentagon contracting pressure. Time, CNN, and Engadget all characterized this as removing the 'flagship safety pledge.' Partially offset by the March 26 Judge Lin court injunction ('Orwellian') restoring federal Claude access — the first full external-accountability-reversal in the AI labs cluster — and documented maintenance of two hard red lines (autonomous weapons, mass domestic surveillance) at confirmed commercial cost (Pentagon May 1 classified-AI cohort exclusion). Net: SYS -0.8, ACT -0.1, ACC +0.2, INT +0.1. Composite 60.0 → 58.1. Boundary case: published 60.0 sat at the Functional/Established pivot; proposed 58.1 moves 1.9pt into the central Functional band. Confidence MEDIUM. Two new methodology categories established: 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' (primary) and 'full-external-accountability-reversal' (contrast to xAI's May 14 partial category).

topSignals.title: India Naval Rohingya Deportation — Second Consecutive Non-Accountability, New Conduct Category
topSignals.description: May 8, 2026: Indian Navy personnel forced 38–40 UNHCR-registered Rohingya refugees — including minors as young as 15, elderly up to 66, and individuals with cancer, TB, heart disease, and diabetes — off a naval vessel into the Andaman Sea with life jackets only. Detained May 6 under the false pretext of biometric data collection, flown to Port Blair, transferred to naval vessel, forced into open water May 8. UN Special Rapporteur: 'unconscionable, unacceptable acts.' India has not confirmed or commented — second consecutive non-accountability for a naval deportation event (May 2025: 36 survivors still in Thailand per Fortify Rights April 9). New conduct category: 'maritime-deportation-without-due-process' — naval-vessel-as-deportation-instrument with sea-as-destination is structurally distinct from land-border or air deportation. Composite 30.5 → 28.1 (-2.4 from working baseline; -6.3 cumulative from published 34.4).

topSignals.title: Meta AI Multi-Jurisdictional Child Safety Convergence — EU DSA Preliminary Breach Finding
topSignals.description: EU Commission preliminarily found Meta in breach of the Digital Services Act for failing to prevent minors under 13 from accessing Instagram and Facebook — a formal regulatory finding, not an investigation. Combined with: US Senate AI child safety bill (bipartisan, passed committee April 30); Hawley Senate investigation (training chatbots to target children with sensual conversation); trial proceedings (child exploitation, grooming, addiction); and leaked internal AI chatbot training rules (Fox News). Four concurrent accountability mechanisms. Partial offset: Meta deployed AI age-profiling (May 2026) under regulatory pressure. Net: EMP -0.1, BND -0.2, ACC -0.3, SYS -0.2, INT -0.2 → 29.4 → 26.3. Cluster pattern with xAI January 2026 CSAM/NCII event confirms AI-chatbot-child-safety-failure-with-multi-jurisdictional-regulatory-pursuit as a sector pattern.

topSignals.title: Russia Death-Toll Revision: 1 to 24 Civilians Killed in Single Overnight Strike
topSignals.description: Euronews confirmed May 15: death toll from Russia's May 14 mass attack rose from 1 to 24 civilians killed. 675 attack drones + 56 missiles in a single overnight package — the largest single-day combined attack of the post-ceasefire period. A 9-story residential building in Darnytsia (Kyiv) collapsed, destroying all 18 apartments. Multi-city strikes: Kyiv, Odesa, Kherson, Kharkiv. 652 drones and 41 missiles intercepted. Macron (May 15): Russia's attack 'laid bare all the hypocrisy with which it negotiated the fragile truce.' New methodology sub-anchor: major-state-bellwether-characterization-of-bad-faith-conduct. Floor sustained at 0.0.

topSignals.title: Google/Alphabet Boundary Case at 40.0 — Developing/Functional Threshold
topSignals.description: Post-hold reassessment of Alphabet/Google (fortune-500, rank 180, composite 40.6) produces a -0.6 delta to exactly 40.0 — the Developing/Functional band boundary. Key evidence: DOJ remedies in active enforcement (choice screen, search index sharing, GenAI/Gemini extended); Judge Mehta denied stay on data-sharing order May 8; DOJ cross-appeal filed seeking Chrome divestiture; 2026 AI Responsibility Report (AGI Futures Council, flood forecasting 2B people); employee 'ethical lapses' reports. INT -0.3, ACT -0.2, EQU -0.1, ACC +0.1, SYS +0.1. Logged per boundary protocol. Note: inter-source discrepancy found — rotation-state shows 51.6 vs index-file 40.6 (11.0pt gap); working from index-file canonical. Math drift (pre-existing): published 40.6 differs from dimensional arithmetic 41.25 by 0.65pt.

newConductCategories.name: safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure
newConductCategories.definition: A frontier AI lab drops a published, structurally load-bearing safety commitment in response to documented external government contracting pressure. Distinct from voluntary policy evolution (no documented external pressure) and external-accountability-induced reform (pressure produces positive change). The canonical test: was a flagship structural commitment reversed, and is the causal role of government pressure documented in primary sources?
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: SYS-dimension primary anchor with high weight. Anthropic RSP v3 (Feb 26) is the canonical instance. The framework's most important signal: if flagship safety pledges can be reversed under procurement pressure, the benchmark's SYS scoring for any lab with government contracts must reflect this risk.

newConductCategories.name: full-external-accountability-reversal
newConductCategories.definition: An entity successfully invokes an external accountability mechanism (court, regulator, independent body) to reverse a government or institutional adverse action, producing a substantive judicial or regulatory finding in the entity's favor. Distinguished from partial-external-accountability-reversal (May 14 xAI category) by: (a) the reversal is complete, not forward-looking; (b) the mechanism produces a substantive finding with institutional weight; (c) the adverse actor's conduct is characterized on-record.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: First full-category external-accountability-reversal in the AI labs cluster. Judge Lin March 26 characterization of Pentagon blacklist as 'Orwellian' is the canonical instance. ACC-dimension half-step upgrade.

newConductCategories.name: perverse-procurement-incentive
newConductCategories.definition: A government contracting process that structurally rewards safety abdication (no restrictions accepted) and punishes safety maintenance (restrictions maintained). Operates at sector level — every lab competing for the affected contract faces the same incentive. Pentagon classified-AI cohort: xAI gained access by accepting no restrictions; Anthropic lost access by maintaining limits.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Cross-cutting sector signal. Should inform SYS/INT scoring for all entities competing in affected procurement pools. The presence of a perverse-procurement-incentive in a sector is itself a SYS-dimension structural negative for the sector's governance environment.

newConductCategories.name: maritime-deportation-without-due-process
newConductCategories.definition: State deportation of refugees using a military naval vessel as deportation instrument, with open sea as the destination. Structurally distinct from land-border or air deportation because: (a) military assets are used for civilian-population removal; (b) the destination is not a defined territory; (c) survivability depends on open-water swimming, not a receiving state's infrastructure; (d) customary non-refoulement (1951 Convention Art. 33) is engaged categorically differently. Near-floor-conduct threshold when vulnerable populations (minors, elderly, terminally ill) are involved.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: India May 2025 + May 2026 events establish this as a recurring conduct category. Methodology should consider whether two annual instances trigger floor-adjacent weighting independent of other compounding evidence.

newConductCategories.name: ai-displacement-paired-mass-restructuring
newConductCategories.definition: Large-scale workforce reduction where senior leadership explicitly attributes the reduction to AI capability gains in public or on-record statements, made in temporal proximity to the layoff event. Multi-dimensional: EMP (workforce harm), EQU (eligibility criteria with age-cohort differential), ACT (proportionality between AI investment and workforce-impact mitigation). Microsoft Rule of 70 (8,750 US buyouts) + Suleyman 'most professional tasks automated in 12–18 months' is the canonical instance.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Distinct from temporary-restructuring (no AI displacement framing) and announced-AI-displacement-without-layoffs (framing without simultaneous conduct). Should be tracked as compound EMP/EQU/ACT signal.

newConductCategories.name: compound-multi-jurisdictional-child-safety-failure-with-internal-source-integrity-signals
newConductCategories.definition: AI platform with documented child-safety governance failure (age-restriction boundary not maintained) facing simultaneous accountability pursuit across four or more distinct institutional mechanisms (EU regulatory body, US legislative, US congressional investigation, civil litigation), combined with internal-source documentary evidence (leaked training rules) of a gap between internal governance documentation and external commitments.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Cluster pattern with xAI January 2026 CSAM/NCII event establishes AI-chatbot-child-safety-failure as a sector pattern. Two sub-categories: (a) product-deployed-with-undisclosed-internal-training-rules (Meta); (b) model-output-failures-at-scale (xAI).

newConductCategories.name: boundary-case-direction-call
newConductCategories.definition: A methodology decision made when a proposed composite sits at an exact band boundary (within 0.5pt), requiring an editorial determination of whether the entity should be placed at the boundary, above it, or below it. The direction call documents: (a) the boundary-proximate evidence basis; (b) the dimensional weights that produced the boundary value; (c) the assessor's recommendation on which band better characterizes the entity's current profile.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Google/Alphabet at 40.0 is the canonical instance. Recommendation: confirm at 40.0 with boundary-case flag; re-assess at next rotation if DOJ/EU/DMA actions resolve. Methodology should establish whether 'boundary confirmation' or 'band assignment' takes priority when evidence is marginal.

newConductCategories.name: drone-as-primary-weapon-paradigm-shift
newConductCategories.definition: When drones exceed 50% of total civilian casualties in a conflict zone over a documented period, this constitutes a structural shift in warfare tactic that changes the dimensional weight of SYS evidence. At 80%+ of casualties (Sudan: 880 of an estimated total killed, January–April 2026), the drone paradigm is fully entrenched — civilian-protection infrastructure cannot assume combatant ground-force restraint norms.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Drone-casualty-percentage as SYS-dimension floor anchor. Should be tracked as a sector-context signal for conflict assessments where drone use exceeds 50% of civilian casualties.

newConductCategories.name: coercive-diplomacy-via-resumption-threat
newConductCategories.definition: A state uses the explicit threat of military resumption as a diplomatic coercion tool during an active ceasefire to compel a specified behavior from the opposing party. Constitutes INT-dimension floor evidence in post-ceasefire contexts — the stated diplomatic posture is to threaten kinetic resumption rather than commit to ceasefire compliance.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Post-ceasefire-specific INT signal distinct from kinetic conduct documentation. Israel May 3 war-resumption threat is the canonical instance. Methodology established in May 14 cycle; formalized here.

newConductCategories.name: third-party-enablement-via-supply-chain
newConductCategories.definition: When a state combatant's conduct of civilian-targeting operations depends materially on a third-party supply chain, the supply-chain actor becomes an adjacent accountability target. Myanmar: Iran-linked 'ghost ship' jet-fuel supply chain for civilian-targeting airstrikes (Amnesty, January 2026) is the canonical instance. The supply chain is documented with specificity sufficient to identify the enabling mechanism.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Adjacent-accountability sub-category of the ACC dimension. The supply-chain actor is documented but not itself assessed in this cycle; the category is applied to the primary actor's record.

floorEntities.conductHighlight: Sector signal scored: Pentagon classified-AI access gained by accepting no restrictions (xAI) vs. lost for maintaining limits (Anthropic) — 'perverse-procurement-incentive' sector category established. Three distinct 2026 Grok governance-failure categories carry forward: CSAM/NCII (January), Musk-protection prompt manipulation (February), unauthorized political-bias modification (May 14). May 15 model retirement (docs.x.ai) is a lifecycle-transparency forward signal. GitHub system-prompt publication pledge (May 14) remains forward-looking, not yet executed. 8+ jurisdictions in active formal regulatory proceedings.

May 14–15 mass attack: 24 civilians killed (Euronews May 15 revised from 1), 675 attack drones + 56 missiles in single overnight package — largest single-day combined attack of the post-ceasefire period. 9-story residential building in Darnytsia (Kyiv) collapsed, all 18 apartments destroyed. Multi-city: Kyiv, Odesa, Kherson, Kharkiv. Macron: attack 'laid bare all the hypocrisy with which it negotiated the fragile truce.' New sub-anchor: major-state-bellwether-characterization-of-bad-faith-conduct. Four-phase post-ceasefire offensive surge confirmed.

May 14–15 airstrikes: Bilin Township (Mon State) 2 killed including child, 6 injured; Mindat (Chin State) 11 airstrikes in 2 days, 4 killed including detainees in custody. HRW World Report 2026: 1,728 civilians killed since late 2024; 1,000+ civilian locations struck in 15 months. Amnesty January 2026: Iran-linked 'ghost ship' jet-fuel supply chain confirmed — third-party-enablement-via-supply-chain category established. Min Aung Hlaing '100-day peace plan' (April 21) vs. continued airstrike conduct = ACC gap event.

UN OHCHR 'high alert' designation active. 880 civilians killed by drones January–April 2026; drones now 80%+ of civilian casualties — 'drone-as-primary-weapon-paradigm-shift' category established. Geographic spread: Kordofan, Darfur, Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum. Airport strike May 4 disrupted all flights. 34 million people (two-thirds of population) require urgent humanitarian assistance; 19 million face acute hunger; 800,000 children expected to face severe acute malnutrition. Both RSF and SAF using drones.

IRC Emergency Watchlist rank 3 globally (behind only Sudan and OPT). Akobo State: 200,000 displaced, 100,000 fled to Ethiopia (cross-border displacement). 73,000 facing starvation and death in Upper Nile/Jonglei. 2.2 million children under 5 acutely malnourished. River aid routes cut off — new access-dimension SYS signal. HRW May 6: 'Civilians starving in South Sudan's conflict areas.' UN ERC Fletcher UNSC warning: 'full-scale famine and collapse' risk.

Cumulative death toll 72,744 (Palestinian MoH, May 14), including 20,179 children. 2,400+ ceasefire violations October 10, 2025 – April 14, 2026. Aid delivery at 37% of allocated trucks (41,714 of 110,400). 1.8M displaced and aid-dependent. Hungary (new Magyar government) pledged ICC warrant enforcement — structural accountability-environment shift. May 3 war-resumption threat documented as 'coercive-diplomacy-via-resumption-threat' INT-dimension signal.

scoreChanges.name: Anthropic
scoreChanges.boundaryWatchResolution: BOUNDARY CASE: Published 60.0 sat at the exact Functional/Established working pivot. Proposed 58.1 moves 1.9pt into the central Functional band — a boundary-departure, not a boundary-cross. Editorial review questions: (a) Is SYS -0.8 the right weight for the canonical safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure anchor? (b) Does ACC +0.2 adequately reflect the full external-accountability-reversal precedent? (c) Does INT +0.1 adequately reflect the documented commercial cost of maintained red lines?

dimensions.rationale: RSP v3 (Feb 26) dropped pre-deployment safety guarantee under documented Pentagon contracting pressure. S1 causal reasoning: reversal occurred under documented external commercial pressure. S2 long-term impact: precedent established that contracting pressure can move pre-deployment safety commitments — systems-level negative for AI safety ecosystem. Canonical safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure anchor.
Mythos Preview limited-partner dual-use deployment without publicly-disclosed safety-evaluation methodology — proportionality drift from Anthropic's prior published-evaluation-first norm.

March 26 court injunction (Judge Lin) restoring federal Claude access after Pentagon blacklist — first full external-accountability-reversal in the AI labs cluster. 'Orwellian' characterization. Half-step upgrade.

Maintained two hard red lines (autonomous weapons, mass domestic surveillance) under Pentagon contracting pressure at confirmed commercial cost (May 1 classified-AI cohort exclusion). Selective integrity — partially positive, offset by RSP drop within same dimension.

evidence.summary: Anthropic published RSP v3 February 26, 2026 — dropped pre-deployment safety guarantee under Pentagon contracting pressure. Time, CNN, Engadget characterized this as removing Anthropic's 'flagship safety pledge.'
Engadget: 'Anthropic weakens its safety pledge in the wake of the Pentagon's pressure campaign.' Confirms causal attribution of the change to government contracting pressure.

Judge Lin granted Anthropic preliminary injunction March 26 restoring federal agency Claude access; characterized Pentagon blacklist as 'Orwellian.' First full external-accountability-reversal in AI labs cluster.
Anthropic continues to refuse to remove restrictions on lethal autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance; Pentagon May 1 classified-AI cohort exclusion confirms documented commercial cost.
Mythos Preview April 7 — cybersecurity dual-use to limited partners without disclosed safety-evaluation methodology. CNBC April 10: Vance/Bessent White House consultation pre-release.
Anthropic RSP v3 — primary-source documentation of the policy change.

keyEvidence: Feb 26, 2026: RSP v3 dropped pre-deployment safety guarantee under documented Pentagon contracting pressure (Time, CNN, Engadget) — first canonical 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' event.
March 26, 2026: Judge Lin court injunction restored federal Claude access; 'Orwellian' Pentagon-blacklist characterization — first full external-accountability-reversal in AI labs cluster.

Pentagon May 1 classified-AI cohort exclusion: Anthropic alone refused to remove lethal-autonomous-weapons and mass-domestic-surveillance restrictions — documented commercial cost of maintained red lines.
April 7 Mythos Preview: dual-use cybersecurity deployment to limited partners without disclosed safety-evaluation methodology.
Net dimensions: SYS -0.8, ACT -0.1, ACC +0.2, INT +0.1 → composite 60.0 → 58.1.
scoreChanges.rationale: SYS -0.8 anchors the assessment: the pre-deployment safety guarantee was the most-cited Anthropic governance differentiator and its removal under Pentagon contracting pressure is a flagship-commitment reversal. The court win is real but cannot offset the loss of a published structural commitment — accountability-mechanism activation is reactive, while pre-deployment safety pledges are proactive structural commitments. Math: (3.6+3.4+3.5+3.1+3.4+3.5+3.1+3.0)/8 = 26.6/8 = 3.325 → ((3.325-1)/4)*100 = 58.125 → 58.1.
watches: RSP v3 implementation details — any further structural-commitment changes
Pentagon contracting posture — DC Circuit oral arguments pending
Mythos Preview public safety-evaluation methodology disclosure (currently absent)
White House carve-out final form — INT forward signal
September 30 Moonshot R&D delivery (postponed from May 15)
Boundary-band volatility: 58.1 sits 2.9pt above 40/41 and 2.9pt below 60/61
carryForward: Boundary-band entity sensitivity to dimensional movement
Pentagon exclusion as INT-positive sector-relative signal
Maintained hard red lines (autonomous weapons, domestic surveillance) under contracting pressure
Court injunction precedent as ACC-positive accountability-mechanism activation

scoreChanges.name: Microsoft
scoreChanges.boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Composite 65.3 in central Established band (61–80), 4.3pt above Functional/Established boundary (60/61) and 14.7pt below Established/Exemplary boundary (80/81). Stable placement.

dimensions.rationale: 8,750 US voluntary buyouts (Rule of 70) + 1,000 LinkedIn cuts (5%) — layoff scale not seen at Microsoft in 51 years. Suleyman 'most professional tasks automated in 12–18 months' reframes as AI-displacement program rather than temporary restructuring. Absence of documented retention/retraining commitments at equivalent scale.
Rule of 70 (age + years of service = 70+) disproportionately affects older workers. Age-cohort differential — EQU-dimension signal.

AC2 proportionality: Microsoft's AI investment scale ($100B+ disclosed May 11 Nadella testimony) paired with this layoff magnitude raises proportionality questions about resource allocation between AI growth and workforce-impact mitigation.

evidence.summary: Microsoft 8,750 US employee voluntary buyout (Rule of 70, age + years of service = 70+) — first time in 51-year company history. May 7 notifications, 30-day window.
Mustafa Suleyman (Microsoft AI division head): 'most professional tasks automated in 12–18 months' — AI-displacement-as-policy framing paired with layoff round.
Nadella testified May 11 in Musk v. Altman: Musk never raised concerns; Microsoft feared OpenAI dependence ('don't want to be IBM'). On-record accountability proceeding participation.

Musk v. Altman closing arguments concluded May 14; jury deliberates Monday May 18. Jury question #3 is aiding/abetting (Microsoft-specific). Advisory verdict only — Judge Gonzalez Rogers decides liability.

keyEvidence: 8,750 US employee voluntary buyout (Rule of 70 — first in 51-year history) + LinkedIn 1,000 (5%) cuts.
Suleyman: 'most professional tasks automated in 12–18 months' — AI-displacement-as-policy framing from senior leadership.
Rule of 70 eligibility (age + years of service ≥ 70) is an age-cohort differential — EQU-dimension signal.
Musk v. Altman jury deliberations Monday May 18; aiding/abetting question (#3) is Microsoft-specific — pre-verdict, not scored.
Net: EMP -0.3, EQU -0.1, ACT -0.1 → composite 66.4 → 65.3.
scoreChanges.rationale: Composite delta -1.1 reflects EMP/EQU/ACT regression from the layoff round paired with AI-displacement-as-policy framing from senior leadership. Microsoft's underlying SYS/ACC/BND infrastructure is sustained. The Musk v. Altman aiding/abetting question is pre-staged but explicitly not scored — verdict landing as early as May 18 may trigger post-verdict ACC/SYS reassessment. Math: (4.0+3.2+3.9+3.2+3.8+3.8+4.0+3.0)/8 = 28.9/8 = 3.6125 → ((3.6125-1)/4)*100 = 65.3.
watches: Musk v. Altman verdict — deliberations Monday May 18; aiding/abetting (Microsoft-specific) could trigger -0.3 to -0.5 ACC/SYS post-verdict
Remedies phase: Altman removal order, $500B restructure reversal, IPO pathway unwinding
Layoff round completion — final headcount impact when 30-day window closes
Suleyman follow-through — workforce retraining/transition program disclosures
carryForward: Strong baseline SYS/ACC infrastructure (carry-over)
Responsible AI office and regulator engagement (carry-over)
Pre-verdict status on Musk v. Altman aiding/abetting (forward signal)

scoreChanges.name: Alphabet/Google
scoreChanges.boundaryWatchResolution: BOUNDARY CASE: Composite 40.0 sits at the exact Developing/Functional band boundary (40/41). Published 40.6 was 0.6pt above; proposed 40.0 is exactly on the boundary. Logged per boundary protocol. Assessor recommendation: confirm at 40.0 with boundary-case flag; re-assess at next rotation if DOJ/EU/DMA actions resolve. MATH-HYGIENE NOTES: (a) Inter-source discrepancy — rotation-state 51.6 vs. index-file 40.6 (11.0pt gap, separate audit required); (b) Pre-existing arithmetic drift — published 40.6 differs from dimensional arithmetic 41.25 by 0.65pt.

dimensions.rationale: DOJ cross-appeal seeking Chrome divestiture indicates DOJ view that remedies-as-ordered are insufficient — external credibility deficit regarding compliance posture. Employee 'ethical lapses' in AI rush (DataCenter Knowledge) — internal-source integrity signals particularly heavy in INT dimension.
AC2 proportionality: Gemini 3 release and AI investment trajectory has outpaced stated AI Responsibility commitments per employee disclosures.
Monopoly conduct as market-power inequity signal. Prior Judge Mehta finding carried forward.
Court-ordered data-sharing mechanism now in active enforcement (Judge Mehta denied stay May 8). Externally imposed; small sub-anchor positive for mechanism operational status.
2026 AI Responsibility Report with concrete deliverables (Gemini 3 safety evaluations, AGI Futures Council, flood forecasting 2B people, AlphaGenome). Positive governance-infrastructure addition.

evidence.summary: DOJ remedies order in effect April 2026: choice screen mandate, search index sharing, no exclusive distribution contracts, extends to GenAI/Gemini products.
Judge Mehta denied Google's stay request on data-sharing order May 8 — enforcement active now.
DOJ cross-appeal filed seeking Chrome divestiture (structural rather than behavioral remedy).
Google 2026 AI Responsibility Report: Gemini 3 safety evaluations, AGI Futures Council, flood forecasting at 2B-person scale, AlphaGenome. Positive SYS signal.
DataCenter Knowledge: Google employees report 'ethical lapses' in AI rush. Internal-source INT signal.

keyEvidence: DOJ remedies order in active enforcement April 2026: choice screen, data sharing, no exclusive contracts, extends to GenAI/Gemini.
Judge Mehta denied stay May 8 — data-sharing order in effect now.
DOJ cross-appeal filed seeking Chrome divestiture (structural remedy pursued).
2026 AI Responsibility Report: Gemini 3 safety, AGI Futures Council, flood forecasting 2B people, AlphaGenome — positive SYS deliverables.

Net: INT -0.3, ACT -0.2, EQU -0.1, ACC +0.1, SYS +0.1 → composite 40.6 → 40.0.
scoreChanges.rationale: Antitrust remedies regime is a mixed signal: court-ordered accountability in active enforcement (ACC +0.1), but DOJ's pursuit of harder structural remedies signals external credibility deficit (INT -0.3), and internal employee disclosures indicate governance integrity not keeping pace with AI deployment scale. Math: (2.8+2.5+2.6+2.4+2.8+2.6+3.0+2.1)/8 = 20.8/8 = 2.6 → ((2.6-1)/4)*100 = 40.0.

AGI Futures Council deliverables (substance behind announcement)

scoreChanges.name: India
scoreChanges.boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Composite 28.1 sits in central-lower Developing band (21–40), 7.1pt above Critical/Developing boundary (20/21) and 11.9pt below Developing/Functional boundary (40/41). However, cumulative -6.3 delta from published 34.4 is approaching material-band-shift territory if compounding evidence continues.

dimensions.rationale: May 8 event: vulnerable population disregard. Minors as young as 15, elderly up to 66, individuals with cancer/TB/heart disease/diabetes forced into open sea. Half-step downgrade reflects vulnerable-population-specificity.

Protection-of-refugees failure with international-law violation. Customary non-refoulement (1951 Convention Art. 33) violated. Naval-vessel-as-deportation-instrument with sea-as-destination is structurally distinct boundary failure.

No government statement, no investigation, no accountability mechanism — second consecutive non-accountability for naval-deportation event (May 2025 + May 2026). Non-accountability pattern now methodologically established at BAU level.

Navy forced 38 UNHCR-registered refugees off naval vessel into Andaman Sea May 8, 2026; included minors (15–16), elderly (up to 66), individuals with cancer/TB/heart disease/diabetes.

Maritime Executive: 'Indian Navy Accused of Forcing 40 Immigrants Into the Sea' — maritime-industry corroboration.

May 2025 incident: 36 Rohingya survivors of prior Indian Navy forced-sea deportation remain in Thailand with no India accountability per Fortify Rights April 9, 2026. Second consecutive non-accountability event.

Deportees detained May 6 under false pretext ('biometric data collection'), flown to Port Blair, transferred to naval vessel May 8.

highlights: Ten new conduct categories in a single cycle — the most consequential methodology harvest since May 14's nine-category night. The two-night run (19 total) represents a structural acceleration in the benchmark's evidentiary taxonomy.

The Anthropic boundary-down call (60.0 → 58.1) is the first documented test of how a frontier AI lab's flagship safety commitment interacts with government contracting pressure — the outcome (RSP pledge dropped; red lines held; court win secured) produces a genuinely mixed evidence structure that the methodology had not previously encountered at this scale.

The Pentagon contracting dynamic now creates a documented structural incentive for safety abdication across the entire AI labs sector. Every lab competing for classified military contracts faces the same tradeoff Anthropic faced. This sector signal should inform SYS/INT scoring for all affected entities going forward.

India's cumulative downgrade since May 6 is -6.3 points (34.4 → 28.1) across two assessment cycles. At this pace, one more comparable compound-negative cycle would place India within 7pt of the Critical band threshold (21.0). The naval deportation cadence (May 2025, May 2026) is now an established annual pattern.

The May 14–15 Russia attack death-toll revision (1 → 24) is methodologically significant: it demonstrates that initial casualty counts in mass-strike events are systematically underestimated, and that revision windows of 12–24 hours are required before treatment as confirmed evidence.

Four boundary-watch entities tonight (Anthropic 58.1, Google 40.0, Agility Robotics 60.9, Apptronik 81.4) represent an unusually high concentration of boundary-proximate risk across three indexes simultaneously.

sectorTrends.trend: Safety governance is bifurcating into two camps: entities that maintain pre-deployment structural commitments under government and commercial pressure (Anthropic — maintained red lines, lost Pentagon access), and entities that abdicate safety constraints in exchange for procurement access (xAI — gained Pentagon classified access by accepting no restrictions). The perverse-procurement-incentive sector signal confirms this bifurcation is now commercially reinforced, not merely ideological. Meta AI's multi-jurisdictional child-safety failure cluster adds a third axis: regulatory accountability for deployed AI products targeting vulnerable populations. Meta + xAI child-safety failures now form a documented sector pattern.

Microsoft's Rule of 70 buyout (8,750 US employees, first in 51-year history) paired with Suleyman's 'most professional tasks automated' framing establishes AI-displacement-paired-mass-restructuring as an EMP/EQU/ACT compound pattern. This may be the first of a wave — as other Fortune 500 companies observe the Microsoft framing, the benchmark expects similar layoff-plus-AI-displacement-attribution patterns in the coming quarter. Watch for: explicit AI-displacement attribution in layoff communications.

Four simultaneous floor entities with compounding evidence: Sudan (world's largest humanitarian crisis, 880 drone-civilian deaths), South Sudan (IRC global rank 3, 73,000 facing starvation), Israel (72,744 cumulative deaths, 37% aid delivery), Myanmar (1,728 civilian deaths, Iran jet-fuel supply chain). The compound nature of these crises — geographic overlap (South Sudan and Sudan share a region), shared enablement dynamics (Iran jet-fuel in Myanmar potentially paralleling drone-supply in Sudan), and simultaneous post-ceasefire conduct patterns (Russia, Israel) — suggests structural interdependence in the floor cluster that the methodology does not yet track systematically.

At 28.1, India is 7.1pt above the Critical band threshold. A third comparable compound-negative cycle would place India in Critical territory. The non-accountability pattern (BAU level per assessor judgment) means the compounding mechanism is self-reinforcing.
emergingRisks.timeframe: Ongoing; next cadence event expected May 2027 per annual pattern

emergingRisks.description: The confirmed structural signal that Pentagon classified-AI access rewards safety abdication creates a sector-wide incentive for AI labs to weaken safety governance in pursuit of military contracts. If additional labs follow xAI's precedent of accepting no restrictions to gain access, SYS/INT scores across the AI labs cluster could systematically decline in coming quarters. The benchmark should add a 'military-contracting-safety-governance' tracking dimension.

emergingRisks.description: UN OHCHR 'high alert' + 'deadlier phase' designation + 880 drone-civilian deaths in 4 months + 800,000 children facing severe acute malnutrition. emergingRisks.risk: Google/Alphabet band boundary — Chrome divestiture structural remedy
emergingRisks.description: Google/Alphabet sits at the exact Developing/Functional boundary (40.0). DOJ cross-appeal for Chrome divestiture, if granted, would be a structural-remedy-level event — the largest single ACC/SYS negative signal possible for an antitrust entity. This could push Google firmly into Developing territory (below 40) or trigger a band-crossing event. Timeline: DOJ cross-appeal proceedings expected 2026–2027.

insights: The Anthropic RSP v3 case is the first time the benchmark has cleanly documented a flagship safety pledge reversal under government contracting pressure in the AI labs cluster. It establishes the 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' category as a SYS-dimension primary anchor, and produces a new methodological distinction between proactive structural commitments (pre-deployment pledges, which can be withdrawn) and reactive accountability mechanisms (court wins, which produce durable precedent but cannot substitute for proactive structural commitments). The SYS -0.8 weight is this cycle's most consequential assessor judgment call — it will set precedent for how the benchmark weights future safety-pledge reversals.

The second consecutive India naval Rohingya deportation (May 2025, May 2026) with non-accountability in both cases is methodologically significant beyond the individual assessment: it establishes that the 'maritime-deportation-without-due-process' category is a recurring conduct pattern, not an isolated event, and that 'accountability-decay' (from May 14 methodology) compounds across cycles for the same event class. The benchmark now has evidence of a one-per-year cadence for this conduct category in a single state, which warrants consideration of whether cadence-of-repeat-conduct should be a separate dimension-level weighting factor.
